_note: Two-plane decomposition for TKT-0003 / ADR-0117 — promoting theme-provider from docs-site chrome (site/lib/theme-provider.ts) to a real shipped ui-theme-provider control. OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the surfaces the shipped component + its migration touch (controls/, catalog, site). INSIDE-OUT actions = what the promoted contract must DO. Crossed via hosts. plan:true — each leaf carries the checkable acceptance predicate the builder's tests must satisfy; no source exists yet under controls/theme-provider/. REVISED post-doc-review (HIGH-1/MEDIUM-1): n17-n19 no longer stand in for the WHOLE migration — a fresh grep found real consumers in ten site/ files, not two; n28-n31 enumerate the rest (the CSS ink-re-root selector, two test files' querySelector calls, prose-only repoints, and the CHANGELOG/llms-full.txt append-only boundary). n25 corrected from a jsdom-mechanism claim to the real browser-test mechanism (jsdom cannot resolve light-dark()/color-scheme).

nodes.label: ui-theme-provider (the shipped component)
tag + package identity
tag ui-theme-provider, class UIThemeProviderElement, folder controls/theme-provider/, extends UIElement
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` — jsdom: `customElements.get('ui-theme-provider')` resolves; the instance is `instanceof UIElement`; `controls/theme-provider/theme-provider.ts` exists and self-defines on import (the fleet self-define precedent)

nodes.label: props schema (scheme / scale / density / theme)
scheme prop — reflected enum ['','light','dark'], default ''
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` — jsdom: unset attribute ⇒ `el.scheme === ''`; `el.scheme = 'dark'` reflects `scheme="dark"`; an invalid attribute value falls back to `''` (fail-open, not a crash and not a fail-closed 'light' coercion)

nodes.label: scale prop — reflected enum ['', the 6 ADR-0032 values], default '' — pure attribute carrier, no JS effect
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` — jsdom: unset ⇒ `el.scale === ''` and no `scale` side-effect fires (no effect reads it); setting `el.scale = 'content-lg'` reflects the attribute only

nodes.label: density prop — reflected enum ['','compact','comfortable','spacious'], default '' — pure attribute carrier
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` — jsdom: same shape as n6 for density's 3-value vocabulary

nodes.label: theme prop — reflected free string, default '' — the RESERVED package seam (ADR-0079 cl.3), pure attribute carrier
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` — jsdom: unset ⇒ `el.theme === ''`; any string round-trips via the attribute; no CSS layer matches any value today (inert by construction, verified by n25's build-output proof touching only scheme)

nodes.label: scheme → color-scheme effect (the unset-inherits fix)
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` — jsdom: `scheme='dark'` sets `el.style.colorScheme === 'dark'`; `scheme='light'` sets `'light'`; `scheme=''` (or the attribute removed after being set) CLEARS `el.style.colorScheme` to `''` (not coerced to `'light'`) so a descendant's computed `color-scheme` inherits from an ANCESTOR context in a nested-provider or page-default test, never collapses to light

nodes.label: theme-provider.css — display:block host rule, no --ui-theme-provider-* token chain, no forced-colors block
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` (family-coherence.test.ts B-group) — the `:where(ui-theme-provider)` block declares only `display: block`; zero `--ui-theme-provider-*` custom properties declared or consumed (the deliberate-absence comment present, the form-provider.css precedent)

nodes.label: theme-provider.md descriptor (tier: container, geometry.sizeClass: container)
nodes.accept: `npm test` — `family-coherence.test.ts` A4 (descriptor exists, tag/class/folder aligned) + the geometry fields resolve `tier: container` / `geometry.sizeClass: container` (the form-provider precedent — a coordination/carrier element with no control height, no --ui-space opinion of its own)

nodes.label: descriptor ↔ static props trip-wire
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` — the `attributes[]` fence block in theme-provider.md mirrors `static props` 1:1 (scheme/scale/density/theme) — the standing per-control trip-wire pattern (code-descriptor.test.ts precedent), red on any drift

nodes.label: catalog disposition
PERMANENT EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entry ('ThemeProvider', ADR-0112 cl.6 precedent) — never catalogue-bound
nodes.accept: `npm test` (a2ui `catalog/default/index.test.ts`) — `ThemeProvider` enters `FLEET_TYPES` (descriptor-derived, ADR-0087) the moment `theme-provider.md` ships; the fleet-coverage gate stays green ONLY because `EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST` carries a `'ThemeProvider'` entry with a recorded reason (page-owner theming chrome, not an agent-emittable content node — the ToastRegion precedent); the residue-guard proves it is never ALSO a catalog key

nodes.label: site migration — zero site-local theme-provider definition remaining
delete site/lib/theme-provider.ts (the local element + its self-define)
nodes.accept: `grep -rL 'customElements.define..theme-provider' site/` finds no site-local definition; `site/lib/theme-provider.ts` no longer exists; every remaining reference in site/ resolves to the shipped `@agent-ui/components` export

nodes.label: theming.ts import + 3 createElement call sites repointed to ui-theme-provider
nodes.accept: `npm run check:site` — `site/pages/theming.ts` imports the shipped control (no local `theme-provider.ts` import); every `document.createElement('theme-provider')`/`code("<theme-provider …")` site becomes `ui-theme-provider`; prose updated where it describes 'a plain, passive site-local wrapper'

nodes.label: component-gallery.ts import + createElement + LLD-C4 comment repointed to ui-theme-provider
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site gallery jsdom suite) + `npm run test:browser` (gallery smoke) — `component-gallery.ts` creates `document.createElement('ui-theme-provider')` in place of `'theme-provider'`; the gallery's scheme/scale/density/theme toolbar still drives the one subtree; the ADR-0079 LLD-C4 interface note is corrected to point at the shipped element

nodes.label: theme-provider-doc.ts — the API doc page (tier=container requires {doc,demo}, site-coverage.test.ts)
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site-coverage.test.ts) — `theme-provider-doc.html` exists and is derived from `theme-provider.md` (attributes/geometry/parts table), the required-page-set check for a `tier: container` component passes

nodes.label: theme-provider-demo.ts — a composition demo using a SELF-COLORING control (ui-button, not bare text — LOW-3)
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site-coverage.test.ts) — `theme-provider-demo.html` exists, satisfying the container tier's `{doc, demo}` required set; the demo shows nested providers AND the unset-inside-a-scheme'd-ancestor case (SPEC-R3 AC4) proven with a real `ui-button`, not bare text (avoids conflating with the separate ink-re-root defect n28 addresses)

nodes.label: gates
jsdom probes — reflect round-trip, unset-inherit fix, pure-carrier attrs, negative controls
nodes.accept: `npm test theme-provider` green, including a negative control that an invalid `scheme` value never renders as a coerced `'light'` style (proving the fix actually bites, not just the happy path)

nodes.label: cross-engine browser probes — scheme/scale/density visibly repaint a REAL nested control
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser theme-provider` (Chromium+WebKit) — a `ui-button` nested under `<ui-theme-provider scheme=dark>` resolves a DIFFERENT background color than under `scheme=light`; a nested control under `[scale=content-lg]`/`[density=spacious]` measures a different box/gap than the default; an unset `scheme` on a provider nested inside a `scheme=dark` ancestor still resolves the ANCESTOR's dark colors (the inherit proof, not just the JS-level style-clear from n9)

nodes.label: BUILT-OUTPUT light-dark() per-subtree resolution proof — TWO tests + a committed fixture (the TKT-0002 regression class — MEDIUM-1 then MEDIUM-A corrected)
nodes.accept: (1) a NEW node-side `theme-provider-build-fixture.test.ts` shells out to `vite build` (reusing `light-dark-minify.test.ts`'s spawnSync + CSS-join mechanics) and asserts the fresh output is BYTE-IDENTICAL to a committed `site/lib/__fixtures__/theme-provider-built.css` (the `llms.test.ts` G1 freshness-gate pattern — a red result names its own fix: rebuild, commit the diff); (2) a NEW `theme-provider-build.browser.test.ts` (Chromium, `test:browser` project) imports that SAME fixture via Vite's `?raw` query (the `app-shell.ts` `ISOLATION_GRID_CSS` precedent — proven real under the browser project, empty under jsdom), injects it into its own live document via a `<style>` element, mounts real `ui-button[variant=soft]`s under `scheme=dark`/`scheme=light` siblings, and asserts `getComputedStyle` resolved INK (`color`, via `--md-sys-color-primary-high`) genuinely differs AND matches an independently-resolved expected token value per scheme (MEDIUM-2 deviation: NOT `variant=solid`/`backgroundColor` — `--md-sys-color-primary` is deliberately scheme-invariant, so that vehicle would have been vacuous-by-construction). Neither test shells out or reads files from WITHIN the browser context (verified: zero `.browser.test.ts` in the repo imports a `node:` module)

nodes.label: barrel/exports/size integration (ADR-0080 marginal pin)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green (barrels.test.ts, exports drift gate); `npm run size` run by hand (ADR-0040 §3 manual discipline) — the leave-one-out marginal for `theme-provider` measured and pinned if it changes the family total materially

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer GO before commit
nodes.accept: a fresh-context `component-reviewer` pass returns GO against the descriptor + anatomy + geometry + contract fidelity (the standing per-control-wave DoD, ADR-0081 remainder)

nodes.label: site/lib/component-gallery.css:45-60 SCHEME-BOUNDARY INK RE-ROOT selector re-keyed to :where(ui-theme-provider) (display:block half moves into theme-provider.css itself, n10)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` (the existing 'theme-provider re-roots inherited ink at the scheme boundary' suite, re-keyed) — a light provider nested in a dark root still gives its bare-text specimens dark ink, not the root's frozen white; the fix is migrated UNCHANGED IN SUBSTANCE, only re-selected onto the new tag

nodes.label: site/gallery.browser.test.ts (2 querySelector call sites, 4 comment/describe mentions) + site/gallery.test.ts (1 querySelector call site) repointed to ui-theme-provider
nodes.accept: `npm test` + `npm run test:browser` — every `gallery.querySelector('theme-provider')` becomes `'ui-theme-provider'` (a missed rename throws on the `as HTMLElement` cast of a null result); describe-block titles and inline comments reworded

nodes.label: prose-only repoints — site/theming.html, site/theming.css, site/main.ts, site/pages/gallery.ts, site/lib/light-dark-minify.test.ts's comment, site/public/llms.txt
nodes.accept: `grep -rnE "(^|[^-A-Za-z/])theme-provider([^-A-Za-z]|$)" site --include=*.ts --include=*.css --include=*.html site/public/llms.txt` (excluding CHANGELOG.md and llms-full.txt, n31; the pattern excludes both `ui-theme-provider`/`controls/theme-provider` AND `theme-provider-doc`/`theme-provider-demo` — MEDIUM-B corrected, the first-pass pattern was unsatisfiable on a correct build) returns zero matches — every comment/prose/blurb mention describes the shipped `ui-theme-provider`, and light-dark-minify.test.ts's OWN assertions/mechanism are untouched (only its comment's file-path reference is corrected, since site/lib/theme-provider.ts no longer exists)

nodes.label: CHANGELOG.md new ratification entry + llms-full.txt regeneration (derived, never hand-edited); historical CHANGELOG entries stay byte-untouched
nodes.accept: `npm test` (`site/lib/llms.test.ts` G1) — `site/public/llms-full.txt` is byte-identical to `node scripts/generate-llms-full.mjs`'s fresh output after the new descriptor + the new CHANGELOG entry land; `git diff CHANGELOG.md` shows only an APPENDED entry — the G8 gallery ship note and the TKT-0002 bugfix note are unmodified

actions.label: identify itself under the fleet ui-* tag law, in the controls/ package tier
accept a scheme override that defaults to 'do not override' (inherit ambient) rather than collapsing unset to light
carry a scale value purely as an attribute for descendant [scale] selectors to key off
carry a density value purely as an attribute for descendant [density] selectors to key off
carry a reserved theme package name, inert today, for a future [theme=] layer to key off
map a live scheme value to its own color-scheme CSS property, and CLEAR that mapping when scheme is unset
lay out as a normal block wrapper and declare no visual token chain of its own
classify itself under the geometry law's Container/layout size-class (no control height, no --ui-space opinion)
mirror its attributes 1:1 in its descriptor (contract trip-wire)
stay permanently out of the generative-UI catalog, with a recorded reason
leave zero site-local theme-provider definition behind
become the theming guide's own living demo element
become the gallery's one theme subtree
give the fleet a discoverable API doc page
give the fleet a composition demo page
prove attribute reflection + the unset-inherit fix under jsdom, including a biting negative control
prove scheme/scale/density visibly repaint a real nested control, both engines, including ancestor-inherit
prove the light-dark() resolution survives through the PRODUCTION build, not just dev mode
stay within the family size budget, exported through the public barrel
pass independent review before shipping
re-root inherited ink at the scheme boundary for bare-text specimens, migrated unchanged onto the new tag
keep every test query pointed at a real, resolvable element
keep every prose/comment reference honest about the shipped tag
record the promotion in the project's forward history without rewriting the past

same class declaration site
same class declaration site
same class declaration site

the CSS scopes to :where(ui-theme-provider) — the tag must be settled before the sheet can target it
the descriptor's attributes[] fence describes the live prop set

the jsdom probes assert the effect's behavior, including the unset-inherit fix
the browser probes assert the same behavior painted, cross-engine
the gallery's real nested controls are the cross-engine paint proof's natural fixture
the dev-mode paint proof must hold before the stricter built-output proof is worth writing
review runs once the gates it grades against are green

review covers the migrated bugfix too
